questions.q: What role did Vasishtha hold in the royal house of Ikshvaku?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the family preceptor (kulaguru) and spiritual guide of the Ikshvaku dynasty of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Vasishtha served as the guru of which king of Ayodhya, the father of Rama?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the royal preceptor and counsellor of King Dasharatha, the father of Rama.

questions.q: To which class of beings is Vasishtha traditionally counted as one of the great sages?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha is reckoned among the Saptarishis, the seven great sages of Hindu tradition.

questions.q: In which city did Vasishtha serve as the royal guru?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the preceptor of the kings ruling from Ayodhya, the capital of the Ikshvaku line.

questions.q: Vasishtha conducted the sacred rites and ceremonies of which royal dynasty?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha performed and oversaw the rites of the Ikshvaku (Raghu) dynasty of Ayodhya.

questions.q: What kind of figure was Vasishtha — a king, a sage, a demon, or a monkey?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was a revered sage (rishi) who served as the spiritual guide of Ayodhya's kings.

questions.q: Whom did Vasishtha primarily counsel and advise at the court of Ayodhya?
options: The king and his royal family
questions.explanation: As royal preceptor, Vasishtha counselled the king and the royal family on matters of dharma and statecraft.

questions.q: Rama and his brothers belonged to the dynasty guided by which guru?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the family guru of the Ikshvaku line to which Rama and his brothers belonged.

questions.q: What is the most fitting description of Vasishtha's relationship to the Ayodhya throne?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the spiritual guide and chief advisor of the kings of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Vasishtha is associated with guiding which famous prince of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the family preceptor of Rama, the eldest prince of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Vasishtha is traditionally said to be a son of which creator deity?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha is described as a mind-born son of the creator Brahma, making him a Brahmarishi.

questions.q: Who was the celebrated wife of Vasishtha, herself revered for her chastity?
questions.explanation: Arundhati, an exemplar of wifely virtue, was the wife of the sage Vasishtha.

questions.q: Vasishtha possessed a wish-fulfilling divine cow that became the cause of a great rivalry. What was her name?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha owned the wish-granting cow Kamadhenu (also called Nandini or Shabala), coveted by King Vishvamitra.

questions.q: Vasishtha's famous lifelong rivalry was with which sage, originally a king?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra, a king who sought Vasishtha's divine cow, became his great rival and later a sage himself.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who principally performed the priestly duties at the marriage of Rama and Sita?
options: Vasishtha and the priests of Ayodhya
questions.explanation: Vasishtha, as the family priest of Ayodhya, officiated at the wedding rites of Rama and Sita at Mithila.

questions.q: When Dasharatha wished to crown a successor, whom did he chiefly consult to arrange Rama's coronation?
options: Vasishtha and the assembly of elders
questions.explanation: Dasharatha consulted Vasishtha and the council of elders to prepare Rama's consecration as crown prince.

questions.q: After Dasharatha's death, what did Vasishtha urge Bharata to do?
options: Seize the throne for himself permanently
Take the kingship since Rama was exiled
questions.explanation: Vasishtha counselled Bharata to assume the kingship as Rama had gone to the forest, though Bharata instead ruled on Rama's behalf.

questions.q: Which honorific best reflects Vasishtha's exalted standing among sages?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha is honored as a Brahmarishi, the highest order of sage, possessing supreme spiritual knowledge.

questions.q: Vasishtha accompanied Bharata's expedition to which place to persuade Rama to return?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha went with Bharata to Chitrakuta, where Rama was dwelling, to entreat him to return to Ayodhya.

questions.q: In the Putrakameshti sacrifice for Dasharatha's sons, what role did Vasishtha play?
options: He oversaw and advised on the sacrifice
He performed it secretly against the king
questions.explanation: Vasishtha, as royal priest, advised Dasharatha and helped arrange the sacrifice to obtain sons, recommending Rishyasringa to conduct it.

questions.q: Vasishtha is regarded as the seer (rishi) of which Mandala (book) of the Rig Veda?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha is traditionally the seer of the seventh Mandala of the Rig Veda, containing his family's hymns.

questions.q: Which great teaching, a dialogue on liberation given to Rama, is attributed to Vasishtha?
questions.explanation: The Yoga Vasishtha is a celebrated philosophical text framed as Vasishtha's teaching of wisdom to the young Rama.

questions.q: When King Trishanku wished to ascend to heaven in his mortal body, Vasishtha's response was to do what?
options: Curse him to die at once
questions.explanation: Vasishtha refused Trishanku's request as against dharma, prompting Trishanku to turn instead to Vishvamitra.

questions.q: The hundred sons of Vasishtha were destroyed by which sage in the course of their feud?
questions.explanation: In the rivalry between the two sages, Vishvamitra brought about the destruction of Vasishtha's hundred sons.

questions.q: Which famous king, said to have offered his own body to redeem his people, was a disciple of Vasishtha?
questions.explanation: The truthful king Harishchandra of the Ikshvaku line is associated with Vasishtha as his family preceptor.

questions.q: In one well-known account, Vasishtha, grief-stricken by the loss of his sons, attempted what, only to be stopped?
questions.explanation: Overwhelmed by grief at his sons' death, Vasishtha tried to take his own life but was each time prevented, and his despair was eventually calmed.

questions.q: The bright star paired with Vasishtha in the sky, named for his wife, is identified with which star of the Great Bear?
questions.explanation: The faint companion star Alcor, near the Saptarishi (Ursa Major) star representing Vasishtha, is traditionally identified as his wife Arundhati.

questions.q: Vasishtha is reckoned the family priest (purohita) across many generations of which solar lineage?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the hereditary preceptor of the Suryavamsha, the Solar dynasty descending from Ikshvaku.

questions.q: According to tradition, after one of Vishvamitra's assaults, Vasishtha's powers were so great that even Vishvamitra acknowledged what?
options: The supremacy of brahminical (spiritual) power over kingly might
That the cow should be slain
questions.explanation: The contest demonstrated that Vasishtha's spiritual (brahmatejas) power surpassed Vishvamitra's kshatriya might, spurring Vishvamitra to seek brahmarishi status through penance.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, after Rama's coronation upon his return, who, as chief priest, is associated with consecrating him as king?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha, the family preceptor, presided over the priestly rites at Rama's coronation (rajyabhisheka) in Ayodhya.
